title: approvals — field classification
description: Approval instances carry two categories of sensitive data: (1) `context_json` + `payload_json` blobs which are polymorphic and may contain fields at any classification tier (a refund's `amount_cents`, an access request's `resource_owner_id`, an impersonation-extension's target user id). Both blobs inherit the STRICTEST classification tier of any nested field — the presenter redacts before wire projection. (2) Approver identities + decision comments are `confidential` — a decision comment on a compliance-approval flow may reveal internal-only rationale. Every template's `when_expression` + `selector_expression` are `internal` (they encode business rules that tenants author, but the strings themselves have no PII).

restricted: ApprovalInstance.context_json (INHERITS STRICTEST tier of nested fields — may carry regulated_financial for money flows, regulated_health for HIPAA flows, regulated_minor for COPPA-adjacent flows)
ApprovalInstance.payload_json (INHERITS STRICTEST tier — the action's replay payload)
regulated_minor: ApprovalInstance.context_json[*] — WHEN the action's context includes a minor's dob / consent-scope / guardian-link (e.g. `sports.athletes.enroll` where athlete is under 18). The action's schema declares the flag; the presenter applies redaction on wire projection to platform-plane admins who don't hold `access.minor_pii`.
regulated_health: ApprovalInstance.context_json[*] — WHEN the action's context includes HIPAA-adjacent PHI (e.g. `compliance.medical_records.export`). Presenter refuses wire projection outside the caller's HIPAA-covered access chain.
regulated_financial: ApprovalInstance.context_json[*] — WHEN the action's context includes PCI-DSS-adjacent card data or amount fields. PCI-DSS requires cardholder data NEVER appear in an approval context — the action's own `approvalContext()` implementation MUST strip PAN / CVV / expiry before returning. The engine trusts the action to comply; violations fire `ApprovalSecurityViolation`.
audit_log: ApprovalExecuted (P0 — this is the moment the tenant state actually changed)
ApprovalExecutionFailed (P0 — signals a downstream bug caused a state drift)
ApproverSelectorEvaluationFailed (P1 — a template rule is broken)

wire_projection.description: Every wire response goes through `ApprovalPresenter`. The presenter (a) applies per-tier redaction based on the CALLER's classifications-visible list from `me.classifications`, (b) resolves the `subject_type + subject_id` polymorphic tuple to a caller-viewable projection (or a redacted stub when the caller lacks access to the underlying resource), (c) redacts `context_json` per the action's `context_variables_schema_json` field-level classification tags, (d) NEVER returns `payload_json` on the tenant plane (that blob is engine-internal — only ApprovalInstanceExecutor reads it), (e) resolves approver_id lists to UserSummaryData for the caller's tenant.

ApprovalInstanceData.subject_type: string (polymorphic type — projected to a caller-viewable stub)
ApprovalInstanceData.subject_summary: SubjectSummaryData | null (redacted stub when the caller can't view the underlying resource)
ApprovalInstanceData.context_summary: Record<string, mixed> (redacted view of context_json per field-level classification tags — key set constrained by action.context_variables_schema_json)
ApprovalInstanceData.can_withdraw: boolean (true when caller is requester AND status='pending' AND template.allows_recall)
ApprovalInstanceData.can_approve: boolean (true when caller is in any pending requirement's resolved_approvers)

ApprovalDecisionData.delegated_from_summary: UserSummaryData | null (present when the approver was acting under an active delegation)
ApprovalDecisionData.comment: string | null (redacted stub when the caller lacks `approvals.decisions.view_comments`)

ApprovalTemplateData.name: string
ApprovalTemplateData.when_expression: string (the raw ExpressionLanguage source — visible to admins on the authoring UI)

ApprovalPreflightResultData.note: Returned by POST /approvals/templates/preflight — dry-run of an expression + selector against a synthetic context. Never persists.

ApprovalPreflightSelectorResult.delegation_substitutions: { from_user_id: string, to_user_id: string, delegation_id: string }[]

ApprovalAuditReportData.note: Returned by POST /platform/approvals/audit-report. SOC 2 CC6.3 + ISO 27001 A.9.2 evidence.

shape.name: string | null
shape.when_expression: string | null (version bump triggered when non-null and different)

shape.context_variables: Record<string, mixed> (synthetic context — the caller supplies values)

shape.comment: string (required; min 10 chars — SOC 2 evidence; max 1000)

output_dtos.purpose: Tenant + platform-plane instance projection. See wire_projection.ApprovalInstanceData.
Template projection for the authoring UI.

notes: The `context_json` blob's classification is DERIVED — its strictest nested field's tier propagates to the whole column. `ApprovalPresenter` computes the effective tier at read time by intersecting `approvable_actions.context_variables_schema_json` (which declares each variable's tier) with `context_json`'s actual keys. Missing schema → treated as `internal` (safe default).

PCI-DSS 3.2.1 Req 3.4: PANs / cardholder data MUST NEVER pass through an approval context. The action's `approvalContext()` implementation is responsible for stripping; the engine trusts the action. Violations detected via a downstream scanner fire `ApprovalSecurityViolation` (P0 audit event).

COPPA / GDPR Art. 6(1)(a) — minor consent tracking: the action's `approvalContext()` MUST NOT include a minor's raw dob or full name; it may include a boolean `is_minor` flag + tenant-scoped athlete_id + consent_scope_id. Guardian identity is referenced by user_id only, never inlined.

The `resolved_approvers` JSONB column on `approval_requirements` is intentionally denormalised — resolving the selector at query time on every read would kill performance on 'pending-my-review' inbox pages. The tradeoff: a delegation activated AFTER the requirement was resolved doesn't retroactively re-materialise; approvers see the snapshot as of instance-create time. `ReconcileTemplateVersionsJob` runs nightly to re-check delegation-touched instances and dispatch supplementary reminders where needed.

`ApprovalDecision.approver_id` MUST match a user_id present in the requirement's `resolved_approvers`. `ApprovalDecisionObserver` refuses inserts that fail this invariant AND fires `ApprovalSecurityViolation` — this closes the direct-DB-write forge attack (someone else's decision recorded against a different user).

`request_ip_hash` is stored as SHA-256 (never plaintext IP) — SOC 2 evidence requires the decision be attributable to a specific origin without persisting the raw address that could reveal the approver's location.
